element_introduction_order.mastery_context: player learns one wrestler's moveset before tournament begins
basic combat fundamentals against single opponent
learning positioning and crowd management with numerical disadvantage
advanced crowd control and sustained offense against overwhelming odds
repeated application of learned disadvantage mechanics at higher difficulty
sustained extreme difficulty requiring mastery of all systems
complete roster mastery - eight wrestlers in sequence, rotating 3-on-1 then 2-on-1 patterns
cartoonish over-the-top moves (Doink's mallet, Razor Ramon's blade arm, Bam Bam's fire fists) learned through experimentation
partner coordination through eight wrestlers in paired-opponent gauntlet

typical_relief_triggers: victory screen with winning wrestler's entrance theme
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: escalating difficulty ladder with rhythmic match-to-match progression; Intercontinental mode gradually introduces handicap mechanics (1v1 → 2v1 → 3v1), then WWF Championship mode repeats and intensifies these patterns, culminating in all-roster gauntlet; each match victory provides brief relief before next opponent spawns

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Adam Bomb hidden character (mentioned in arcade version but incomplete in home ports; presence not indicated spatially)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Intercontinental Championship mode: four introductory one-on-one matches establish baseline combat against each opponent type; relatively forgiving
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Intercontinental Championship transitions to 2-on-1 handicap matches (2 total), requiring crowd management and sustained aggression; moderate difficulty spike
difficulty_curve.late_game: Intercontinental Championship climaxes with single 3-on-1 match; WWF Championship mode entirely consists of handicap matches (4× 2-on-1, 2× 3-on-1) escalating to WrestleMania Challenge gauntlet requiring defeat of all eight wrestlers in sequence with rotating handicap configurations
notable_spikes: transition to WWF Championship mode (all matches are handicap format)
WrestleMania Challenge gauntlet (eight opponents, cumulative fatigue, no breaks)

notable_level_patterns.description: Two single-player modes structured as linear tournament ladders: Intercontinental Championship (7 matches) and WWF Championship (7 matches). Early matches introduce mechanics, later matches repeat and intensify them.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Intercontinental Championship mode: matches 1-4 are 1v1, matches 5-6 are 2v1, match 7 is 3v1

notable_level_patterns.description: Difficulty increases through opponent multiplication rather than individual opponent strength. Matches progress from 1v1 → 2v1 → 3v1 format, with each spawned opponent replacing eliminated allies.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: 3-on-1 match: player faces three simultaneous opponents; when one is defeated, a replacement enters from opposing corner

notable_level_patterns.description: WrestleMania Challenge requires defeating entire eight-character roster in sequence with rotating handicap ratios (3v1 initially, transitioning to 2v1), without health reset between matches.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: WWF Championship mode final challenge: eight wrestlers appear in predetermined order, cumulative damage carries across all eight matchups

notable_level_patterns.description: Two-player cooperative mode mirrors single-player gauntlet structure but with paired opponents and two-player teams facing groups of two from roster.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Tag Team Champions mode: four two-on-two matches progressing through all eight wrestlers

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (developer/platform info, SNES port specifics)
wikipedia - WWF WrestleMania: The Arcade Game (gameplay modes, character roster, match structure, difficulty progression)
